source_jobs.title: User Acquisition Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Joko, we help consumers shop smarter. Our mission is to revolutionize shopping, empowering people to find what they need, make informed decisions, and save money.

Founded in Paris, Joko is a tech company and certified B Corp with over 105 talents across Paris, Barcelona, and New York (and beyond). More than 6 million users already save money every day at 10,000+ merchants with Joko.

From cashback and automatic coupons to price alerts and carbon tracking, we keep expanding our products to make shopping smarter. We’re now building an AI-powered shopping assistant to help users find the best products by price, quality, and environmental impact.

Having reached profitability in our core market, we’re now scaling globally, with a strong focus on the US.

It’s still day 1, come build the future of shopping with us!




✨ OUR MARKETING TEAM

No one is more creative and data-driven than this team - they have the best of both worlds!

The Marketing team’s main mission is to get our product into the hands and hearts of millions of users around the world.

 - The User Growth department builds innovative ideas to conquer markets, attract, and retain users.

 - The Brand & Communication department strives to create Joko’s vision for the long term and builds a connection with all its users.

 - The Campaign & Merchandising department drives our Joko Business-to-Consumer campaign calendar and enhances the customer shopping experience across our merchandising assets.

 - The B2B Marketing department positions Joko as the top partner for retailers’ marketing teams, and aims to build the most desired employer brand possible.

Led by Isa, our CMO, they are constantly innovating to take our business to new heights.



🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DO

As User Acquisition Manager, you will define and implement Joko’s user acquisition strategy across all markets and platforms (mobile app and web). Your goal will be to grow Joko’s user base and maximize user value. You will be part of the Growth department within the Marketing team and report directly to the Head of Growth.

Your responsibilities will be:

 - Drive strategic thinking in user acquisition: Take part in the broad strategic thinking around how we drive user growth and make the best use of our acquisition budget across all potential levers.

 - Optimize acquisition profitability and budget allocation: Own and refine the channel mix and investment strategy to maximize the LTV/CAC ratio. Continuously monitor performance and reallocate spend to the most profitable levers.

 - Manage online paid advertising campaigns: Plan, launch, and ptimize campaigns across channels (Social Media Ads, Ad Networks, Apple Ads, Google etc.). Deep dive into performance data and market practices to identify, test, and implement growth opportunities.

 - Drive creative excellence: Thoroughly analyze ad performance to derive actionable insights and guide iterations. Partner with our internal Design and Social Media teams, as well as external resources (creators, tools, AI solutions) to continuously test and produce top-performing creatives efficiently.

 - Maximise our App Stores performance (ASO) through continual benchmarking and testing in order to maximize our visibility and conversion on the app and browser extension stores.

 - Contribute to building a world-class team of UA experts, by staying up to date with all industry’s latest developments including ads channels’ features and market best practices, and making sure Joko stays ahead of UA innovations

 - Work hand-in-hand with other teams such as Product, Data and CRM to ensure consistency across the user funnel and coordinate efforts in reaching the company’s growth ambition.




👀 WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: You have 2y+ successful experience in B2C mobile app paid acquisition and ASO and you fully master user acquisition platforms, tools, and OS technicalities.

 - Languages: You are a native-level French speaker and are fluent in English both writt

source_jobs.title: Account Manager (Card-Linked Business)
source_jobs.description:
At Joko, we help consumers shop smarter. Our mission is to revolutionize shopping, empowering people to find what they need, make informed decisions, and save money.

Founded in Paris, Joko is a tech company and certified B Corp with over 105 talents across Paris, Barcelona, and New York (and beyond). More than 6 million users already save money every day at 10,000+ merchants with Joko.

From cashback and automatic coupons to price alerts and carbon tracking, we keep expanding our products to make shopping smarter. We’re now building an AI-powered shopping assistant to help users find the best products by price, quality, and environmental impact.

Having reached profitability in our core market, we’re now scaling globally, with a strong focus on the US.

It’s still day 1, come build the future of shopping with us!



This position is not remote-friendly and is Paris office-based.




☎️ OUR B2B TEAM

“Nothing stops them from getting the best deals for Joko users. The B2B team’s mission is to develop and grow Joko’s merchant network, and available offers.”

The B2B team is divided between our three business lines at Joko: Online, Card-Linked, and Gift Cards.

 - For the Online team, Account Managers are in charge of growing and managing our portfolio of e-commerce partners, directly with e-merchants or through our relationship with affiliate marketing platforms.

 - As for Card-Linked, Account Executives are in charge of signing Enterprise deals with top-tier retailers with physical stores, and Account Managers are in charge of growing these long-lasting partnerships.

 - Finally, for the Gift Cards team, Account Managers are in charge of growing and managing the portfolio of brands and retailers, through relations with providers and in direct relationships with merchants.

Led by Nicolas, our CRO, we are a vibrant team full of energy! We work closely with other teams to give the best experience to our partners and build strong long-term relationships. We are a small but unstoppable squad that manages millions of euros in contracts thanks to our outstanding team members and a deep sense of camaraderie. We don't take ourselves too seriously and make sure that the journey is a blast!




🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an Account Manager (Card-Linked), your main mission will be to increase Joko’s turnover by maintaining and developing our existing partnerships with top-tier retailers in the French market. You will be in charge of your own portfolio of partners, and work closely with other teams such as Sales, Business Operations, Product, Data, and Marketing.

You will work on our CLO (card-linked offers) business line, and report directly to the Card-Linked Account Strategist Lead.

Your responsibility will be to help CMOs grow their business thanks to Joko, and generate tens of millions of incremental revenue. This includes:

 - Allowing your partners to reach their target audience and acquire new customers thanks to our powerful payment data targeting;

 - Leveraging payment-data-based analysis to gather a clear vision of your partners’ market trends, and use this knowledge to perfectly tailor your offer proposal;

 - Helping your partners build their brand awareness among more than 3m+ people using Joko for everyday spending, by working with the marketing team;

 - Becoming a trusted partner helping them define their global marketing strategy;

 - Creating strong and long-lasting relationships with your partners (day-to-day relationship management, securing regular formal and informal touchpoints -meetings, live events…)

You will also work to make the Card-Linked team grow:

 - Working with the Product and the Bizops teams in order to improve processes and build solid foundations to scale our department at Joko, in France, and abroad;

 - Contributing to the design of the commercial and onboarding strategy.




👀 WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Preferred experience: You have 2-5y experience in Finance, Consulting or Startups.

 - Education

👀 WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Track record: You've worked with real production constrain

source_jobs.title: Offer Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
At Joko, we help consumers shop smarter. Our mission is to revolutionize shopping, empowering people to find what they need, make informed decisions, and save money.

Founded in Paris, Joko is a tech company and certified B Corp with over 105 talents across Paris, Barcelona, and New York (and beyond). More than 6 million users already save money every day at 10,000+ merchants with Joko.

From cashback and automatic coupons to price alerts and carbon tracking, we keep expanding our products to make shopping smarter. We’re now building an AI-powered shopping assistant to help users find the best products by price, quality, and environmental impact.

Having reached profitability in our core market, we’re now scaling globally, with a strong focus on the US.

It’s still day 1, come build the future of shopping with us!






🛠️ OUR OFFER OPERATIONS TEAM

We're building Offer Operations as a new team at Joko — and this is a foundational hire.

Joko helps millions of users earn cashback automatically, whether they shop online through affiliate links, activate coupons, or pay in-store with their linked bank card. Behind each of these experiences, there's an operational engine that needs to work flawlessly: transactions must be tracked, cashback must be caught, offers must be reliable, and merchants must perform as expected. When this engine breaks, users don't get paid — and trust erodes fast.

Offer Operations will own that engine. The team's mission is to guarantee the reliability and continuous improvement of Joko's core monetization mechanics — across affiliate tracking, coupons, card-linked bank integrations, and merchant performance. It's a technical, data-heavy, high-stakes scope that sits right at the heart of what makes Joko's product work.




🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DO

As Offer Operations Lead, you'll work directly with Quentin https://www.linkedin.com/in/quentin-chaleard-5a602931/, our COO, to build this function from the ground up. This is not a maintenance role — it's a founding mandate. You'll shape what Offer Operations becomes at Joko, starting with building a team and the runway to grow.

Your core mission: make Joko's offer operations reliably excellent. Concretely, that means:

 - Deliver on the metrics that matter. You own track rates, catch rates, offer reliability, and merchant performance. When something breaks, your team finds it, fixes it, and makes sure it doesn't happen again.

 - Build and lead the team. You'll recruit, coach and develop 2 Ops Managers from day one, set the bar high, and hire as the scope grows.

 - Drive continuous improvement. Processes, tooling, monitoring — much of it is still being built or improvised. You'll bring structure where there's none and improve what already exists, from quick wins to long-term scalable solutions.

 - Be the operational voice in product decisions. You'll work closely with Product & Engineering to flag issues, shape the roadmap from an ops perspective, and make sure operational needs don't get lost in feature priorities.

 - Drive merchant performance and partner with the commercial team. Many operational issues originate at the merchant level — broken tracking, unreliable offers, inconsistent data. You'll build the frameworks to monitor and score merchant performance, identify underperformers, and drive corrective actions. You'll work hand in hand with the commercial team to turn operational insights into partner conversations: escalating issues, setting quality standards, and making sure merchant reliability improves over time — not just gets patched.

 - Define how this team operates. There's no playbook — you'll set the rhythm, the rituals, and the standards. OKRs, sprints, escalation processes: you decide what this team needs to run well.




👀 WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: You have at least 5 years of experience, with meaningful exposure to Product, Operations, or a technical/data-driven environment.

 - Builder mindset: You have an entrepreneurial str

Led by Fabien, our VP Product, Joko's Product team groups the passionate individuals behind our users' delight! From brainstorming to development to launch, the Product team's main mission is to turn ideas into reality by working hand-in-hand with the Engineering, Design and Business teams.

Joko is organized into four product tribes, each owning a key dimension of the company's value:

 - Business lines owns the current core value proposition, covering online shopping, in-store experiences, and gift cards, each run as a dedicated team.

 - Scale focuses on growing and amplifying this value through growth, international expansion, and shared core user experiences across the app.

 - Joko AI builds the next value proposition: a shopping assistant powered by advanced AI and new interaction models, combining deep technical and product innovation.

 - Internal needs serves teams and non user-facing stakeholders, owning internal tools, infrastructure, compliance, and operational scalability.




🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DO

As Joko's first dedicated Growth Product Manager, you will have the unique opportunity to shape what growth product means at Joko from the ground up. Reporting to Fabien (VP Product), your mission is to generate growth across usage and monetization — owning the full AARRR funnel and operating as one of the most cross-functional PMs in the company.

 - Growth strategy: You will define and execute a product strategy to drive acquisition, activation, retention, revenue, and referral. You will identify the highest-impact levers and build the roadmap to activate them.

 - Onboarding: You will own the user onboarding journey — from app download to completed setup (desktop extension, bank linking, MSE install) — and continuously improve conversion at every step across mobile, web app, and browser extension.

 - Joko AI: Joko AI — our shopping assistant powered by advanced AI and new interaction models — is the next core pillar of our value proposition. As soon as it ships in the product, the Growth task force will own its onboarding and activation: designing how new and existing users discover it, try it, and turn it into a daily habit. You will be at the forefront of bringing AI to millions of shoppers.

 - Experimentation: You will run a high cadence of A/B tests to improve conversion and engagement across the funnel. With Joko's user volumes, even a 2% conversion lift translates into meaningful impact — and you will own that process end to end.

 - Referral & retention: You will drive the referral program and lead initiatives to reduce churn, one of Joko's key improvement areas.

 - Cross-functional collaboration: You will work hand-in-hand with Marketing (growth & CRM), the Business Line PMs (Online, Gift Cards, Card-Linked), the Joko AI tribe, the Data team, and a dedicated Product Designer embedded in your task force.

 - Revenue: You will contribute to monetization initiatives, identifying how to better convert and extract value from the existing user base.

The Talent team exists because we believe talent is the single biggest asset of a company, and that identifying, attracting, and closing the best profiles is a craft worth investing in seriously. ✨ WHY THIS ROLE IS SPECIAL

Design at Joko has been a founding obsession. From day one, we've believed that a B2C product lives or dies on the quality of its experience, and we've held an unusually high bar on craft, motion, and detail across every surface of the app.

Three things make this seat rare:

 - You inherit a real design culture, not a blank page. Joko already has a strong design DNA, a mature design system, and a product team where craft is non-negotiable across the board. You're not starting from zero, you're inheriting a high baseline and pushing it further.

 - You get to define what design at Joko becomes in the AI era. The way digital products are designed is changing fast. We are actively rethinking interaction models for our AI shopping assistant, and we want a design leader who has strong opinions on what world-class B2C design looks like when the interface itself is becoming conversational, generative, and adaptive. You will shape that, not just react to it.

 - You will live a genuinely unique scale moment. Going from a small design team to 7 Product Designers and a User Researcher, while expanding from France to Spain and the US, is a rare kind of stretch for a design leader. You get to do it here, with a product that already has the user base, the revenue, and the ambition to justify the investment.




🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DO

As Head of Product Design, you will own the vision and execution of Product Design at Joko. Reporting to Fabien (VP Product), you will be hands-on early, then build and scale a high-performing Design and Research team as we grow. Your mission is to raise the bar on craft, coherence, and user-centricity across every Joko product.

 - Own the design vision and quality bar: You will define what great design means at Joko, the principles behind it, and the standards every shipped surface is held to. You will be the person the team looks to when there is a hard call to make on craft.

 - Stay hands-on where it matters: Especially early on, you will be deep in discovery, flows, UI, and interaction design on the highest-stakes problems. You know that a Head of Product Design who has lost touch with the work has lost the plot.

🧲 OUR TALENT TEAM

At Joko, hiring has been a founding obsession since day one. Our founders have always been convinced that you don't crack a market with a big team, you crack it with a small team of exceptional people. That bet has worked: we've grown to 6 million users and become one of France's most recognized shopping apps with just 30 people in the early years, and we've kept the same bar as we've scaled past 100.

The Talent team exists because we believe talent is the single biggest asset of a company, and that identifying, attracting, and closing the best profiles is a craft worth investing in seriously. We are constantly experimenting with state-of-the-art techniques, tools, and processes to find the people who will build the future of shopping with us.

Today, the Talent team sits within the broader People function, which also covers People Ops, People Business Partnering, and Workplace across France, Spain, and our growing international footprint into the US. We are a small team with a big scope, and we move fast.




✨ WHY THIS ROLE IS SPECIAL

Recruiting is Joko's #1 company objective, and as a Talent Acquisition Manager you sit right at the heart of it. Three things make this role genuinely rare:

 - The team is exceptionally involved in hiring. From ICs to managers to founders, recruiting is treated as a first-class responsibility, not something delegated to TA. You will work with hiring partners who actually show up, give sharp feedback, and care about the bar as much as you do. Your debriefs will be real conversations, not rubber stamps.

 - You will have the tools, and the budget, to be on the frontier. We already work with a modern stack (Ashby, Claude, Claap, and more), and we keep investing to stay among the most performant and modern TA setups out there. You will not be fighting for basic tooling — you will be using the best of what exists, and contributing to deciding what comes next, including how we deploy AI across the funnel.

 - You will live a genuinely unique scale moment. Going from 100 to 250 (and more) with a real international component (France, Spain, US, and more to come) is the kind of stretch most recruiters only get to do once. You get to do it here, with a company that has already proven it can build a recognized product with a tiny team — and with full ownership of the roles you run.




🎯 WHAT YOU WILL DO

As a Talent Acquisition Manager, your mission is to attract, evaluate, and close the talent that will take Joko from 100 to 250 people, with the bar, craft, and intentionality that have defined Joko's talent density since day one. You will report to Déborah https://www.linkedin.com/in/deborah-rippol-7b741512/?skipRedirect=true (VP People) and partner closely with hiring managers across all functions — engineering, product, sales, marketing, ops, and more.
